_note: Slab c.1300-1180 BC — the LEGENDARY royal houses of the Trojan War. Every figure is mythological and flagged LEGENDARY (no historical figures here; dates are nominal placeholders, each child's year smaller than its parent's). Prefixes: MYC_ = the Pelopid House of Atreus + the Spartan Tyndarids (Mycenae/Argos/Sparta); TRO_ = the new house 'Troy & the Dardanians'; deities reuse the existing CRE_ set (CRE_ZEUS already in the tree; CRE_APHRODITE new). THREAD 1 — HOUSE OF ATREUS: Atreus (existing MYC_ATREUS, son of Pelops) > Agamemnon (over-king of Mycenae, led the Greek host to Troy) & Menelaus (king of Sparta). Agamemnon x Clytemnestra > Orestes, Iphigenia (sacrificed at Aulis), Electra, Chrysothemis. Menelaus x Helen > Hermione. Thyestes (existing MYC_THYESTES) > Aegisthus, who seized Clytemnestra & the throne (the usurper) and with her murdered Agamemnon. THREAD 2 — SPARTAN TYNDARIDS: Tyndareus x Leda > Clytemnestra, Helen, Castor & Pollux (the Dioscuri). Received myth splits the paternity: Zeus came to Leda as a swan the same night as Tyndareus, so Helen & Pollux are counted Zeus's (divine, immortal) and Clytemnestra & Castor Tyndareus's (mortal) — versions differ, and the divine liaison is drawn as the childless union CRE_ZEUS x Leda; Helen & Pollux carry the swan note in their labels. Helen & Clytemnestra are thus both Leda's daughters, married to the two Atreid brothers. THREAD 3 — TROY & THE DARDANIANS (new TRO_ house, the Dardanian line): Zeus x Electra (the Pleiad) > Dardanus (founder) > Erichthonius > Tros (eponym of Troy/the Troad) > Ilus (founder of Ilion/Ilium), Assaracus & Ganymede (cup-bearer of the gods); Ilus > Laomedon > Priam (last king of Troy) & Hesione. Priam x Hecuba > Hector (x Andromache > Astyanax, the child thrown from the walls), Paris/Alexandros (who carried off Helen — the union CRE... TRO_PARIS x MYC_HELEN links the two houses & the war's cause), Cassandra (the unheeded prophetess), Deiphobus, Helenus (the seer), Polyxena, Troilus & others (Priam is said to have had ~50 children; only the famous ones drawn). The junior Dardanian branch: Assaracus > Capys > Anchises, who lay with the goddess Aphrodite (CRE_APHRODITE) > AENEAS, who fled burning Troy carrying his father — the LEGENDARY ancestor of the Romans (his son Ascanius/Iulus founded Alba Longa; the Julii claimed descent). NOTE FOR A FUTURE SLAB: Aeneas > (Ascanius) > the Alban kings > Romulus links this Trojan house forward to the founding of Rome (ANTIQUITY house). Web-verified against Wikipedia/Theoi 'Dardanus', 'Tros', 'Ilus', 'Laomedon', 'Priam', 'Hecuba', 'Hector', 'Aeneas', 'Anchises', 'Aphrodite', 'Tyndareus', 'Leda', 'Helen of Troy', 'Dioscuri', 'Agamemnon', 'Menelaus', 'Aegisthus'; where sources diverge (Leda's paternity split, Aegisthus's incestuous birth) the labels note the variant. No fabrication — received mythology followed.

families.note: SPARTAN TYNDARIDS (LEGENDARY) — Tyndareus x Leda, parents of the war's two fateful women (Helen & Clytemnestra) and the Dioscuri. Root couple; the divine paternity of Helen & Pollux is drawn separately below.

a.label:
👑 Tyndareus
c.1300 BC · king of Sparta; husband of Leda; father of Clytemnestra & Castor, mortal foster-father of Helen & Pollux; his oath of the suitors bound the Greek kings to Menelaus · LEGENDARY

b.label:
Leda
c.1298 BC · queen of Sparta; wife of Tyndareus; visited by Zeus in the form of a swan the same night, she bore Helen, Clytemnestra & the Dioscuri · LEGENDARY

children.label:
Clytemnestra
c.1275 BC · daughter of Tyndareus & Leda; queen of Mycenae, wife of Agamemnon; with her lover Aegisthus she murdered Agamemnon on his return from Troy · LEGENDARY

Helen of Troy
c.1275 BC · daughter of Leda by Zeus (the swan) — 'the face that launched a thousand ships'; wife of Menelaus, carried off by Paris to Troy, the cause of the war · LEGENDARY

Castor
c.1276 BC · son of Tyndareus & Leda (the mortal twin); horseman & one of the Dioscuri, twin brother of Pollux · LEGENDARY

Pollux (Polydeuces)
c.1276 BC · son of Leda by Zeus (the immortal twin); boxer & one of the Dioscuri; shared his immortality with his mortal twin Castor · LEGENDARY

families.note: SPARTAN TYNDARIDS (LEGENDARY) — the swan: Zeus (existing deity) x Leda, the divine liaison that fathered Helen & Pollux. Childless union shown to mark the myth; the children are drawn above under Tyndareus & Leda.
HOUSE OF ATREUS (LEGENDARY) — Atreus (existing, son of Pelops) to the two brother-kings who led the war: Agamemnon of Mycenae & Menelaus of Sparta. Their mother is Aerope.

b.label:
Aerope
c.1315 BC · Cretan princess, granddaughter of Minos; wife of Atreus; mother of Agamemnon & Menelaus · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Agamemnon
c.1290 BC · son of Atreus; over-king of Mycenae/Argos; supreme commander of the Greek host at Troy; sacrificed Iphigenia at Aulis; slain by Clytemnestra & Aegisthus on his return · LEGENDARY

👑 Menelaus
c.1288 BC · son of Atreus; king of Sparta; husband of Helen, whose abduction by Paris launched the war · LEGENDARY

families.note: HOUSE OF ATREUS (LEGENDARY) — Agamemnon x Clytemnestra (Leda's daughter, defined above) and their children.

children.label:
👑 Orestes
c.1255 BC · son of Agamemnon & Clytemnestra; avenged his father by killing Clytemnestra & Aegisthus; pursued by the Furies, later king of Mycenae/Argos · LEGENDARY

Iphigenia
c.1258 BC · daughter of Agamemnon & Clytemnestra; sacrificed (or spirited away by Artemis) at Aulis to raise the winds for Troy · LEGENDARY

Electra
c.1256 BC · daughter of Agamemnon & Clytemnestra; kept faith with the dead Agamemnon & spurred Orestes to avenge him · LEGENDARY

Chrysothemis
c.1257 BC · daughter of Agamemnon & Clytemnestra; the compliant sister who, unlike Electra, submitted to the usurpers · LEGENDARY

families.note: HOUSE OF ATREUS (LEGENDARY) — Menelaus x Helen (Leda's daughter, defined above) and their daughter Hermione.

children.label:
Hermione
c.1258 BC · daughter of Menelaus & Helen of Sparta; later wife of Orestes (and, in some tellings, of Neoptolemus) · LEGENDARY

families.note: HOUSE OF ATREUS (LEGENDARY) — Thyestes (existing, brother-rival of Atreus) to Aegisthus. Received myth makes Aegisthus the son of Thyestes by his own daughter Pelopia; the incest is noted in the label rather than drawn.

b.label:
Pelopia
c.1300 BC · daughter of Thyestes; by her own father she bore Aegisthus — the incest that continued the curse of the House of Pelops · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Aegisthus
c.1285 BC · son of Thyestes (by his daughter Pelopia); seized Clytemnestra & the throne of Mycenae — the usurper; with her murdered Agamemnon, and was later slain by Orestes · LEGENDARY

families.note: HOUSE OF ATREUS (LEGENDARY) — the usurpation: Aegisthus took Clytemnestra (wife of the absent Agamemnon) as consort & ruled Mycenae with her. Union only; together they killed Agamemnon.

TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — the founder: Zeus (existing deity) x Electra the Pleiad > Dardanus, who founded Dardania on the Troad. (This Electra is the Pleiad, distinct from Agamemnon's daughter MYC_ELECTRA.)

b.label:
Electra
c.1400 BC · the Pleiad, daughter of Atlas; by Zeus she bore Dardanus, founder of the Trojan royal line · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Dardanus
c.1380 BC · son of Zeus & Electra; founder of Dardania on the Troad & the ancestor of the Trojan kings; eponym of the Dardanelles · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Dardanus to Erichthonius, said to be the richest of mortal men.

b.label:
Batea
c.1378 BC · daughter of Teucer, first king of the Troad; wife of Dardanus; mother of Erichthonius · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Erichthonius
c.1360 BC · son of Dardanus; king of Dardania, said to be the wealthiest of men, lord of three thousand mares; father of Tros · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Erichthonius to Tros, who gave his name to Troy and the Trojans.

b.label:
Astyoche
c.1358 BC · daughter of the river-god Simoeis; wife of Erichthonius; mother of Tros · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Tros
c.1340 BC · son of Erichthonius; king who gave his name to Troy (Troia) & the Trojans; father of Ilus, Assaracus & Ganymede · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Tros to his three sons: Ilus (the senior/royal line), Assaracus (the junior line toward Aeneas) & Ganymede.

b.label:
Callirrhoe
c.1338 BC · daughter of the river-god Scamander; wife of Tros; mother of Ilus, Assaracus & Ganymede · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Ilus
c.1320 BC · son of Tros; founder of Ilion/Ilium (the city of Troy proper); father of Laomedon; the senior royal line · LEGENDARY

👑 Assaracus
c.1318 BC · son of Tros; king of Dardania & head of the junior Dardanian branch that runs Capys > Anchises > Aeneas · LEGENDARY

Ganymede
c.1316 BC · son of Tros; the most beautiful of mortals, seized by Zeus (as an eagle) to be cup-bearer of the gods on Olympus · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Ilus to Laomedon, the treacherous king who cheated Apollo, Poseidon & Heracles.

b.label:
Eurydice
c.1318 BC · daughter of Adrastus; wife of Ilus of Troy; mother of Laomedon (distinct from the Perseid Eurydice of Mycenae) · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Laomedon
c.1300 BC · son of Ilus; king of Troy who built its great walls with Apollo & Poseidon then cheated them & Heracles; father of Priam & Hesione · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Laomedon to Priam (the last king of Troy) & Hesione (given to Telamon of Salamis when Heracles sacked Troy).

b.label:
Strymo
c.1298 BC · daughter of the river-god Scamander; wife of Laomedon; mother of Priam & Hesione · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Priam
c.1280 BC · son of Laomedon; the last king of Troy; father of Hector, Paris & some fifty children; slain by Neoptolemus at the city's fall · LEGENDARY

Hesione
c.1278 BC · daughter of Laomedon; rescued from a sea-monster by Heracles then given to Telamon of Salamis; mother of Teucer the archer · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Priam x Hecuba and the famous children (of the ~50 attributed to Priam).

b.label:
Hecuba
c.1278 BC · chief queen of Troy, wife of Priam; mother of Hector, Paris, Cassandra & many more; her grief became the archetype of the fall of Troy · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Hector
c.1250 BC · eldest son of Priam & Hecuba; heir of Troy & its greatest warrior; killed Patroclus, was slain by Achilles & dragged round the walls · LEGENDARY

Paris (Alexandros)
c.1248 BC · son of Priam & Hecuba; his abduction of Helen began the war; awarded the golden apple, he slew Achilles with an arrow · LEGENDARY

Cassandra
c.1246 BC · daughter of Priam & Hecuba; prophetess cursed by Apollo to foresee truly yet never be believed; taken as spoil by Agamemnon · LEGENDARY

Deiphobus
c.1252 BC · son of Priam & Hecuba; a leading Trojan warrior who married Helen after Paris's death; killed at the sack of Troy · LEGENDARY

Helenus
c.1246 BC · son of Priam & Hecuba; seer & twin of Cassandra; survived the war & later ruled in Epirus, wedding Andromache · LEGENDARY

Polyxena
c.1244 BC · daughter of Priam & Hecuba; sacrificed on the tomb of Achilles after the fall of Troy · LEGENDARY

Troilus
c.1242 BC · young son of Priam & Hecuba; his death at Achilles' hands was fated to seal Troy's doom; hero of the later Troilus & Cressida legend · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Hector x Andromache and their son Astyanax, thrown from the walls at the city's fall.

b.label:
Andromache
c.1250 BC · wife of Hector; princess of Cilician Thebe; after Troy fell she was taken by Neoptolemus, later wed Helenus · LEGENDARY

children.label:
Astyanax (Scamandrius)
c.1225 BC · infant son of Hector & Andromache; hurled from the walls of Troy by the Greeks so no heir of Hector would survive · LEGENDARY

families.note: CROSS-HOUSE LINK (LEGENDARY) — the cause of the war: Paris of Troy carried off Helen (Menelaus's wife, Leda's daughter). Union of the two royal houses; drawn without issue (their children died in infancy in most tellings).
TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — the junior branch: Assaracus to Capys.

b.label:
Hieromneme
c.1316 BC · daughter of the river-god Simoeis; wife of Assaracus; mother of Capys · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Capys
c.1300 BC · son of Assaracus; king of Dardania; father of Anchises · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Capys to Anchises, beloved of the goddess Aphrodite.

b.label:
Themiste
c.1298 BC · daughter of Ilus & sister of Laomedon; wife of Capys; mother of Anchises · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Anchises
c.1280 BC · son of Capys; Dardanian prince & herdsman of Mount Ida; loved by Aphrodite, to whom he fathered Aeneas; borne from burning Troy on his son's shoulders · LEGENDARY

families.note: TROY & THE DARDANIANS (LEGENDARY) — Anchises x the goddess Aphrodite > AENEAS, who fled Troy and became the legendary ancestor of the Romans. (Aphrodite is a new deity node; grouped with the existing CRE_ deity set alongside Zeus.) FUTURE-SLAB LINK: Aeneas > Ascanius/Iulus > the Alban kings > Romulus ties this house forward to the founding of Rome (ANTIQUITY house).

b.label:
Aphrodite
c.1300 BC · goddess of love & beauty; loved the Dardanian prince Anchises & bore him Aeneas, as told in the Homeric Hymn to Aphrodite · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Aeneas
c.1250 BC · son of Anchises & the goddess Aphrodite; Dardanian hero who fled burning Troy carrying his father; the LEGENDARY ancestor of the Romans — his son Ascanius/Iulus founded Alba Longa & the Julii claimed his line · LEGENDARY
